title: Node 24, TypeScript 7.0.2, TypeScript 6 API Compatibility, npm, and Reproduc… · Workflow Trust Lens: Ci Reproducibility Changed Environment
summary: A TypeScript build uses floating Node and npm versions, npm install mutates the lockfile, lifecycle scripts gain network authority, and local generated files hide a clean-run failure. Planned artifact: Add and verify the ci reproducibility changed environment increment in reproducible TypeScript build contract. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Repeat installs and builds across cold cache, supported Node versions, locale, timezone, architecture, and changed dependency conditions.
Build from a clean checkout, exclude local residue, declare generated inputs, and compare packaged output with source and ignore rules.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# cicd-github-actions cumulative delivery-system portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic workflow, policy, artifact, and release evidence review only.
# They never call GitHub APIs, start runners, execute learner commands, access Docker or registries, exchange OIDC tokens, or contact cloud services.

version-baseline:
  checkout: 7.0.0
  setup-node: 7.0.0
  upload-artifact: 7.0.1
  download-artifact: 8.0.1
  attest: 4.1.1
  node: 24-lts
  typescript-native: 7.0.2
  typescript-api-compatibility: 6.0.2
  build-push-action: 7
  google-auth: 3
  deploy-cloudrun: 3
transfer-boundary: authorized-disposable-delivery-environment

steps.title: Predict Ci Reproducibility Changed Environment · Theory Ci Reproducibility Changed Environment
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory TypeScript build floats Node and npm, mutates its lockfile, runs dependency lifecycle scripts, calls a programmatic API that TypeScript 7 does not provide, and passes only with warm local residue. Prove Node 24, the TypeScript 7.0.2 native CLI, the TypeScript 6.0.2 compatibility API, npm ci, clean output, and changed-environment behavior. Competency probe: Repeat installs and builds across cold cache, supported Node versions, locale, timezone, architecture, and changed dependency conditions.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: A worked example TypeScript build floats Node and npm, mutates its lockfile, runs dependency lifecycle scripts, calls a programmatic API that TypeScript 7 does not provide, and passes only with warm local residue. Prove Node 24, the TypeScript 7.0.2 native CLI, the TypeScript 6.0.2 compatibility API, npm ci, clean output, and changed-environment behavior. Competency probe: Repeat installs and builds across cold cache, supported Node versions, locale, timezone, architecture, and changed dependency conditions. A defensible solution applies Ci Reproducibility Changed Environment, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to reproducible TypeScript build contract.

content.code:
# Evidence: ci-worked-ci-node-typescript-reproducibility-3
  - competency: ci-worked-ci-node-typescript-reproducibility-3
    artifact: typescript-build.yaml
    decision: worked ci node typescript reproducibility 3
    environment: Node 24 TypeScript monorepo with three lockfiles
    fault-injection: expire the only retained test artifact during incident reconstruction
    retained-proof: accessible redacted timeline with owner expiry and regression control
    case-key: worked3
    change: |
      node: 24-lts
      npm: 11-or-later
      typescript-native: 7.0.2
      typescript-api-compatibility: 6.0.2
      tsc: native-compiler
      api-tools: typescript6-alias
      setup-node: 7
      install: npm-ci-frozen-lock
      package-manager-cache: risk-reviewed
      workspace: clean-checkout
      build-output: content-digested
      changed-environment: cold-cache-and-locale
    verify: npm ci then run strict type test build and package checks from a clean supported Node 24 environment
    failure: Reject floating toolchains, npm install lock mutation, local residue, lifecycle-script trust, or one warm build as reproducibility.
    repair: Align tool versions, freeze installs, bound registry and scripts, build cleanly, digest output, and repeat changed environments.
